content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Adolph Donins 
1931 North H street, #67 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
December 21, 1989 
Re: 
Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-682 
Dear Mr. Donins: 
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act") .11 
Your request is 
one for general assistance. 
Accordingly, we are treating it as a 
request for informal assistance. 21 
QUESTIONS 
Which employees of the State Compensation Insurance Fund (the 
"Fund") are required to file statements of economic interests? 
Where are those statements located, and are they of public record? 
CONCLUSION 
The conflict of interest code for the Fund is currently on 
file at the offices of the Fair Political Practices commission. 
As stated in the conflict of interest code, the original financial 
disclosure statements for the board of directors are forwarded to 
our offices. 
However, original statements for all additional 
designated positions, and copies of the board of directors' 
statements, are filed at the Fund's offices in Sacramento. 
Pursuant to section 81008, every statement of economic interests 
filed pursuant to the Act is a public record and open for public 
inspection and reproduction. 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
21 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. section 
18329 (c) (3) .) 
the 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Mr. Donins 
December 21, 1989 
Page 2 
I have enclosed a copy of the conflict of interest code for 
the Fund. 
To obtain copies of statements of economic interests 
for designated employees, you should contact the agency directly 
at its Sacramento office. 
If you are interested only in the board 
of director's statements, you may contact our Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662 for assistance. 
FACTS 
You have been referred to the offices of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission to ascertain if the employees of the State 
Compensation Insurance Fund are filing disclosure statements, and 
if so, whether you can obtain copies from our office. 
ANALYSIS 
Conflict of interest codes must be promulgated by all state 
agencies. 
They must designate those positions within the agency 
which make or participate in the making of decisions which may 
have a foreseeable material financial effect on any financial 
interest. 
For each designated position, the code must describe 
the types of financial interests made reportable. 
To determine which employees are designated and what 
disclosure requirements they have been assigned, please refer to 
the enclosed copy of the conflict of interest code for the Fund, 
complete with the appendix of designated employees and the 
appendix of disclosure categories. 
I hope that the above has been helpful to you. 
If you have 
any questions, or would like to discuss this further, please feel 
free to call me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:CAH:I89682 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
(,ftc'tl1' Ct ft:JCt-~ 
I 
By: 
Cheryl A. Hoff 
Staff Services Analyst 
Legal Division 
cc 
State Compensation Insurance Fund 




California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Adolph Donins 
1931 N. "H" street, #67 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Dear Mr. Donins: 
December 1, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-682 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on November 29, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Cheryl Hoff, in the Legal Division, directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you 
as to the information needed. 
If your request is for informal 
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See 
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
)
